title: Quercus neglecta
taxonomy: Quercus neglecta is a species of oak tree that belongs to the family Fagaceae. It is a part of the genus Quercus, which comprises over 600 species of deciduous and evergreen trees. The genus Quercus is further divided into several subgenera, including the subgenus Quercus, which includes many species of white oak. Quercus neglecta is also a member of the section Lobatae, which includes various species of white oak native to North America. The classification of Quercus neglecta is still being studied by botanists, and more research is needed to fully understand its relationships within the Quercus genus.

intro: Quercus neglecta, also known as the Forgotten Oak, is a species of tree in the Fagaceae family, characterized by its unique combination of oak-like and beech-like characteristics. The leaves and bark of Quercus neglecta have been traditionally used in medicine, boasting a range of health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of arthritis, improve cardiovascular health, and support immune function. The bioactive constituents of Quercus neglecta include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and tannins, particularly quercetin and 3,4-dihydroxybenzoic acid, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Quercus neglecta is commonly prepared as a tea, tincture, or extract, and is used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues, digestive problems, and skin conditions.
